llm_result.summary: The case shows a new-parent, postpartum distress characterized by altered self-perception since childbirth (C01), strong positive bond with the baby (C02) coexisting with overwhelm, guilt, tearfulness, and perceived entrapment (C03-C07), and concern about validation from others (C08). Modifiable predictors span sleep biology, emotion regulation, self-compassion and identity work, CBT-based cognitive-behavioral skills, social support, and mindfulness-based approaches. The proposed levers are practical, non-pharmacologic, and targeted to reduce distress by improving sleep, coping capacity, self-relief from guilt, sense of autonomy, and social validation, while leveraging interactions among BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains to maximize impact within a 4–12 week horizon. Each lever links to measurable and actionable outcomes and includes safety considerations for escalation if noted risk emerges.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured sleep improvement plan including consistent bedtime and wake time, daytime nap management, optimized sleep environment (dark, quiet, comfortable), light exposure/darkness cues, and sleep diary tracking with a weekly review.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep stabilizes mood regulation, reduces emotional reactivity (tearfulness), and diminishes guilt amplification by increasing cognitive bandwidth for parenting tasks.
solutions.measurement: Weekly sleep diary (duration, awakenings), Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) score, days with ≥7 hours sleep.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep deprivation risk; refer to clinician if insomnia persists or worsens; non-pharmacologic sleep aids only.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_POSTPARTUM Sleep optimization mapped to C05 (overwhelm), C06 (tearfulness), C07 (self-criticism)

solutions.solution_candidate: A brief CBT/DBT-inspired skills program focusing on identifying emotions, cognitive reappraisal, distress tolerance, and urge surfing; 4–6 modules with at-home practice and weekly check-ins.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced ability to label and modulate emotions reduces reactive crying, self-criticism after emotional episodes, and perceived overwhelm by providing coping-based responses.
solutions.measurement: ERQ (Emotion Regulation Questionnaire) or DERS short form; weekly counts of emotional episodes; self-rated regulation quality.
solutions.safety_notes: No medication involved; monitor for escalation of distress; adjust support if crying persists with functional impairment.
solutions.mapping_query: PSYCHO: emotion regulation training mapped to C04, C06, C07, C05

solutions.solution_candidate: Compassion-focused self-talk and self-forgiveness; 6-week guided exercises (e.g., compassionate letters, self-kindness prompts, and mindful self‑compassion routines).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing self-criticism after emotional episodes decreases guilt amplification (C04, C07) and supports re-integration of postpartum identity (C01) while enhancing coping capacity (C05).
solutions.measurement: Self-Compassion Scale (short), frequency of self-critical thoughts, subjective sense of identity coherence.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for mood flare if distress increases; provide supportive supervision as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: PSYCHO: self-compassion training mapped to C01, C04, C07, C05

solutions.solution_candidate: Identity-focused work to harmonize maternal role with personal self through values exploration, role redefinition, and future-oriented meaning-making; 3–4 sessions plus journaling.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Clarifying and integrating postpartum identity reduces distress from altered self-perception and improves engagement in parenting and personal goals.
solutions.measurement: Identity integration scales; pre/post self-report on meaning and coherence of self-concept.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for identity-related rumination or distress; adjust pace accordingly.
solutions.mapping_query: PSYCHO: identity consolidation mapping to C01

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured plan to mobilize practical support: involve partner/family, arrange limited respite/childcare, and enroll in postpartum support groups; include care navigation to access resources.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increased practical and emotional support reduces entrapment (C03), perceived overwhelm (C05), and validates distress (C08).
solutions.measurement: Number of supportive contacts per week; respite hours per week; social support scales (MSPSS).
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure boundaries and caregiver safety; coordinate with partner and family to prevent burnout.
solutions.mapping_query: SOCIAL: social support planning mapped to C03, C05, C08

solutions.solution_candidate: Hybrid CBT approach targeting parenting guilt and depressive-like rumination, plus behavioral activation to re-engage in daily routines; include small, graded tasks.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Cognitive restructuring reduces self-blame (C04) and guilt (C07); behavioral activation alleviates overwhelm by increasing functioning and routine (C05).
solutions.measurement: Thought records; activity scheduling adherence; weekly monitoring of task completion and mood.
solutions.safety_notes: Non-pharmacologic; monitor for worsening mood; escalate if suicidality emerges.

solutions.solution_candidate: Mindfulness/ACT-inspired practices to cultivate present-moment awareness, acceptance of distressing emotions, and values-driven action; 8–12 weeks with guided practice.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances tolerance for distress, reduces self-judgment post-emotional episodes, and supports adaptive actions in line with values, addressing identity strain (C01) and overwhelm (C05).
solutions.measurement: FFMQ (short form); distress tolerance tasks; weekly values-aligned actions log.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety risk; ensure appropriate support for emotional overwhelm.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured psychoeducation about postpartum distress and validation strategies; clinician/coach feedback to counter minimization by others; practical scripts for seeking support.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly addresses perceived minimization and invalidation, enhancing validation and helping the individual feel understood.
solutions.measurement: Validation perception scale; satisfaction with clinician/coach feedback; distress rating.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; provide supportive resources if distress increases.
